title: Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night

notes: Using the GOG version: doesn't seem to detect controllers properly through Steam (and keyboard binds are unbindable and awful), but works well through Lutris. Always takes about 8 seconds for the title screen to first detect input, but works fine after. Performance is decent, although the game hangs up a lot whenever it has to build shaders (when a new character or enemy shows up, or during almost any new screen transition). Borderless Fullscreen seems to have best performance. Won't let you alt-tab properly, but doesn't choke and die if you attempt it. Having Esync enabled is possibly slightly better than disabled, but messing with D3D11 tweaks just seems to break lighting. Crashes very occasionally.
title: Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night

notes: Okay, so it also works on Manjaro. Still need to change the screen setting to "Borderless Full Screen." However, there is a bug where occasionally the screen size will shrink, particularly in Big Picture Mode. It only happens in Manjaro, and not in Ubuntu. Also, the Cloud Save for this game is broken, I'm not able to continue my saved game on different computers. Otherwise, it runs and plays just fine.
title: Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night

notes: Seems to run perfectly
title: Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night

title: Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night

notes: The game runs perfectly out of the box without any issue. I was able to maintain a stable 120 fps on 1080p max settings with minor shader compilation stutters. Disable "Framerate Smoothing" to run at framerates higher than 60. However, there are 2 minor issues after closing the game: an error message appears saying d3d11 could not initialize fullscreen, and the game leaves a process running after closing, which must be killed manually. However, these are minor things that do not affect gameplay.

every time and Steam sees the game as still running and I have to kill the process manually.

notes:
Runs perfectly out of the box. I didn't notice a single crash or drop in framerates. Initially there was no rumble on my Steam Controller, but running Xbox controller emulation in SC-Controller fixes that. I used this mod to add 21:9 support:
https://community.pcgamingwiki.com/files/file/1324-bloodstained-ritual-of-the-night-ultrawide-multimonitor-mod/
It's a really awesome game, I can't recommend it enough. It has a sword with an integrated steam engine, guys!
